questions.text: Tell me about yourself.
Walk me through your resume.
Why are you interested in this role, and why now?
Why this company over others you are talking to?
Where do you see yourself in five years?
What are you looking for in your next role that you do not have today?
What questions do you have for me about the role or the team?
What is your greatest strength, and how has it shown up in your work?
What are you better at than 90 percent of your peers?
What would your last manager say is the most valuable thing you brought to the team?
Tell me about the accomplishment you are most proud of.
What is a weakness you have actively worked on improving?
Tell me about a time you failed. What did you take away from it?
Tell me about a piece of feedback that was hard to hear. What did you do with it?
What is something you would do differently if you could go back to your last role?
Tell me about a time you led a project from start to finish.
Tell me about a time you had to make a decision without enough information.
Tell me about a time you had to resolve a conflict with a colleague.
Tell me about a time you had to influence someone more senior than you.
Tell me about a time you had to push back on a decision you disagreed with.
Tell me about a time you had to work with someone who was very different from you.
Tell me about a time you went above and beyond your job description.
Tell me about a time you took on something outside your area of expertise.
Tell me about a time you had to deliver a project under a tight deadline.
Tell me about a time you handled a high-pressure situation. What kept you grounded?
Tell me about a time you turned an unhappy customer or stakeholder around.
Tell me about a time you spotted a problem before anyone else did.
Tell me about a time you had to make an unpopular decision.
Tell me about a time you mentored or coached someone. What changed for them?
Tell me about a time you had to recover from a public mistake.
Tell me about a time you had to manage your own time across competing priorities.
Tell me about a time you had to step into a leadership role you were not formally given.
Tell me about a time you had to make a trade-off between speed and quality.
Tell me about a time you had to advocate for something you believed in against pushback.
Tell me about a time you adapted quickly to a major change at work.
How do you handle stress when multiple priorities collide?
How do you handle disagreement with someone you respect?
How do you keep learning in a field that moves quickly?
Describe the kind of manager you do your best work for.
Why are you leaving your current role?
